feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: WHO dementia prevention guidelines update: What changed?
entry.summary: Up to 45% of dementia cases globally could be delayed or even prevented by acting on modifiable risk factors, new WHO guidelines show.

entry.title: FDA raised conflict of interest concerns ahead of new peptide panel
entry.summary: That panel will meet to weigh whether the agency should allow Americans to have broader access to seven injectable peptides previously restricted over potential safety concerns.

entry.title: A tick-borne disease has no warning rash and doctors say it's spreading across the U.S.
entry.summary: Ticks are spreading new diseases faster than ever—and one virus family may already be evading our immune system.

entry.title: She moved closer to home for her aging father. Then, a surprise
entry.summary: Natalie Bryan's dad was diagnosed with Alzheimer's in 2024. She thought it would change everything, but he hasn't declined at all thanks to treatment.

entry.title: Doctors reveal hidden danger for some Ozempic, Wegovy users with brain disorders
entry.summary: For an ALS patient, losing weight can cause the condition to progress faster.

entry.title: The CDC Has a Cyclospora Lab. DOGE Downsized It Last Year
entry.summary: “Based on simple math, these outbreak responses—which require rapid, timely responses—are going to be greatly diminished,” the former CDC lab director tells WIRED.

entry.title: How Portland restaurants are responding to the cyclosporiasis outbreak
entry.summary: These restaurateurs are confident that they can keep their customers and staff safe during a national surge in cases of the gastrointestinal infection.

entry.title: What does Code Red air quality mean? Weather Talk explains
entry.summary: Experts recommend people reduce outdoor activities as air quality worsens because of Canadian wildfire smoke.

entry.title: A Pitt professor breaks down what to know about the diarrhea-causing cyclosporiasis
entry.summary: False

entry.title: Exclusive | Bill Essayli calls for investigation after rare cancer diagnoses rattle Orange County families
entry.summary: An outbreak of an extremely rare cancer in a ritzy SoCal community has raised the concern of Los Angeles’ top federal attorney.

entry.title: Ebola spreading more quickly in DRC, while Uganda is close to being virus-free
entry.summary: Uganda ⁠⁠discharges last-remaining patient, as WHO says Ebola has ‘expanded faster than any previous outbreak’ in DRC.

entry.title: Dementia Prevention Guidelines Updated—Here’s What Changed
entry.summary: The new health guidance marks the first major update since 2019.

entry.title: The “Explosive Diarrhea” Outbreak May Come Down to a Familiar Culprit. This Keeps Happening for a Reason.
entry.summary: Our salad days are well and truly on hold.

entry.title: Ancient DNA Finally Solves a 16th-Century Murder Mystery
entry.summary: The Grand Duke Francesco de Medici, a prominent politician in the Tuscany region of Italy in the 16th century, died under mysterious circumstances in 1587 – with the suddenness of his death giving rise to rumors that he'd been poisoned.

entry.title: Only 12% of testosterone prescriptions are medically necessary — and Trump wants to make that ratio even smaller
entry.summary: Washington is loosening access as demand surges, blurring the line between treatment and lifestyle upgrade.

entry.title: John Cena opens up about 'eye mites,' the tiny bugs causing his blurry vision
entry.summary: John Cena reveals the "parasite" causing his blurry vision and red eye symptoms. He's taking proactive steps to protect his health as he approaches 50.

entry.title: Brain Activity Noise Linked to Autism Communication
entry.summary: A new study utilizes 128-sensor EEG to prove that altered aperiodic neural noise predicts autism communication challenges.

entry.title: Women Are Sharing Super Helpful Things Their Gynecologists Taught Them That People Don't Know
entry.summary: "You can ask for a small speculum."

entry.title: Second rare cancer strain emerges as Southern California cluster grows
entry.summary: The Ladera Ranch Maintenance Corporation has said it is creating an advisory committee made up of homeowners, board members, staff and landscaping professionals to review its landscaping practices …

entry.title: Two cases of ‘explosive diarrhea’ parasite confirmed in Knox County
entry.summary: KNOXVILLE, Tenn. (WATE) — The Knox County Health Department has confirmed two cases of cyclosporiasis, the parasite capable of causing serious gastrointestinal symptoms, including “explosive” diarrhea. KCHD said one was reported in May and the second in June.…

entry.title: Mom gives birth in ambulance after not knowing she was pregnant
entry.summary: A Massachusetts mom was overjoyed to bring her first baby home from a hospital NICU five months after a surprise pregnancy and delivery.

entry.title: Breakdown of immune cells' interaction is key driver in aging, study finds
entry.summary: We may age at different rates, but none of us escapes aging. A study in mice and human cells by Stanford Medicine researchers pins much of the blame on a particular type of immune cell's increasing inability, with advancing age, to gobble up another immune ce…

entry.title: Long Sleep Hours Link to Elevated Alzheimer’s Blood Protein
entry.summary: A new study uses non-linear splines to prove long sleep links to elevated plasma p-tau181 biomarkers.

entry.title: The US military will now test for ‘low T.’ Here’s what your testosterone levels actually tell you
entry.summary: Make sure the number on a testosterone check opens a real evaluation of health instead of becoming a shortcut to a prescription, Dr. Jamin Brahmbhatt advises.

entry.title: Dietitians Say This Is What You Should Eat for Breakfast If You Want All-Day Energy
entry.summary: Discover the foods dietitians recommend to fuel your body, avoid afternoon slumps, and maintain steady energy from morning to night.

entry.title: Paralysed man regains hand function through novel brain technology
entry.summary: Patient went from not being able to lift his hands to his face to independently wiping his mouth

entry.title: Cyclospora outbreak: Hamilton County reports additional cases
entry.summary: As cyclosporiasis continues to spread to more counties and states, Hamilton County is reporting more cases.

entry.title: Can nicotine make you smarter? Why biohackers — and conservative pundits — are embracing the controversial habit
entry.summary: “Nicotine is one of the most misunderstood compounds in the biohacking world,” biohacker Dave Asprey told The Post.

entry.title: Nanoparticles Cross Blood-Brain Barrier in Mice to Shrink Deadly Glioblastoma
entry.summary: Cancers don't come much worse than the brain cancer glioblastoma, and it is notoriously difficult to treat.

entry.title: How to avoid tick bites in your yard or while hiking in the woods, and what to do if one starts feasting on you
entry.summary: A tick disease researcher explains the risks and how she avoids bringing home ticks from both work and walking her dog in the woods. Lint rollers, tick checks and some types of chemicals play a role.

entry.title: She Used Weight-Loss Drugs From a Telehealth Company, Then a GLP-1 Overdose Sent Her to the ER
entry.summary: A North Carolina woman says she was prescribed a weight-loss drug from a telehealth company and suffered a GLP-1 overdose after following their dosage instructions.

entry.title: Skin Issues Can Signal Heart Problems. Here’s the No. 1 Sign to Watch For
entry.summary: Your skin isn’t separate from the rest of your body, a dermatologist says. And it can even clue you into potential heart health issues. Here’s what to look for.

entry.title: Scientists show how sweeteners slow the growth of certain gut bacteria
entry.summary: Cambridge researchers have shown how commonly-used sweeteners slow the growth of certain gut bacteria.

entry.title: Geriatrician reveals the habit the healthiest people over 80 always avoid
entry.summary: The healthiest people in their 80s and 90s have one surprising lifelong habit in common. Here’s why embracing the cringe may help keep your body and brain sharp.

entry.title: What is cyclospora? Diarrhoea symptoms, foods linked to US outbreak
entry.summary: Cyclospora cases are rising across the US. Here’s what to know about symptoms, spread, treatment and prevention.

entry.title: A Vaccine to Prevent Pancreatic Cancer in High-risk Individuals Was Safe and Elicited Durable Immune Responses
entry.summary: After a median follow-up of 16.5 months, none of the participants developed pancreatic cancer, and some of the precancerous lesions shrank or stopped growing.

entry.title: Case of cyclosporiasis confirmed in Hawaii visitor
entry.summary: In general, state health officials say the threat level remains low for Hawaii residents.

entry.title: Cyclospora in Maryland: Cases more than double since last week
entry.summary: Cyclosporiasis cases in Maryland have more than doubled since last week, state health officials said.

entry.title: Ketogenic diet mediates intestinal tumorigenesis through lipids not ketones
entry.summary: A combination of different manipulations in mouse models of spontaneous intestinal adenoma formation dissects the roles of systemic and epithelial ketogenesis versus dietary lipids in ketogenic diet-mediated intestinal tumorigenesis, highlighting th…

entry.title: Scientists Have Identified a Key Driver of Age-Related Cognitive Decline
entry.summary: A newly identified protein pathway may link blood-brain barrier deterioration to age-related cognitive decline.

entry.title: Biologists pinpoint how common virus triggers multiple sclerosis
entry.summary: A connection between Epstein–Barr virus and MS has long been suspected, but the underlying mechanism was unclear until now.

entry.title: 11 surprising habits that are increasing your biological age
entry.summary: From not eating enough carbs to exercising too much, our expert explains where you may be going wrong

entry.title: Blood Test Accurately Predicts Five-Year Alzheimer’s Risk
entry.summary: The p-tau217 blood test predicts a 38% risk of cognitive impairment within 5 years.

entry.title: A Sensible New Yorker’s Guide to Legionella Season
entry.summary: With the latest Legionnaires’ outbreak in New York City, we talked to Columbia University’s Dr. Wafaa El-Sadr about whether you can drink tap water (yes) and whether you need to wear a hazmat suit walking past the Met (probably not).

entry.title: New study untangles how Epstein-Barr viral infection triggers immune response in multiple sclerosis
entry.summary: A new study claims to have uncovered how Epstein-Barr virus launches immune responses seen in people with multiple sclerosis.

entry.title: Cassidy to Trump public health nominee: ‘Why would you repeat those damn lies?’
entry.summary: Sen. Bill Cassidy (R-La.), chair of the Senate Health Committee, grew angry with President Trump’s nominee to lead the Administration for Strategic Preparedness and Response (ASPR) during his confirmation hearing on Wednesday over a misleading social media po…

entry.title: Number of UES buildings with positive Legionella tests more than doubles since preliminary findings
entry.summary: One of these buildings is likely responsible for an outbreak of Legionnaires’ disease that has sickened 60 people so far, city health officials say.

entry.title: How to protect yourself from the diarrhea-causing parasite wreaking havoc across the US
entry.summary: The parasite is borne by fresh produce and bagged salads and can cause watery diarrhea for days. Here’s what doctors say you can do to prevent getting sick.

entry.title: Cell-type signatures of Alzheimer’s disease shared across population groups
entry.summary: Single-nucleus RNA-seq and ATAC-seq analyses on post-mortem brain samples from African American, Latin and white individuals identify cell-type-specific molecular signatures that are associated with cognitive impairment and/or Alzheimer’s disease across diver…

entry.title: Ammonia pressure controls colloidal metal nitride synthesis in molten salts
entry.summary: A molten salt, high-pressure synthesis method enables solution-based production of diverse refractory metal nitride nanocrystals, expanding access to technologically important nitride materials for advanced applications.
